title.zh: 卷三十三 朱馮虞鄭周列傳
title.en: Volume 33: Biographies of Zhu, Feng, Yu, Zheng, Zhou
title.raw: 卷三十三 朱馮虞鄭周列傳 Volume 33: Biographies of Zhu, Feng, Yu, Zheng, Zhou

sentences.zh: 朱浮字叔元，沛國蕭人也。

translations.literal: Zhu Fu, courtesy name Shuyuan, was a man of Xiao in the Pei kingdom.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Fu, styled Shuyuan, came from Xiao in the state of Pei.

sentences.zh: 初從光武為大司馬主簿，遷偏將軍，從破邯鄲。

translations.literal: At first he followed Guangwu as clerk of the Grand Marshal, was promoted to general of the flank, and followed him in capturing Handan.
translations.idiomatic: He first served Emperor Guangwu as secretary to the Grand Marshal, rose to general of the flank, and took part in the reduction of Handan.

sentences.zh: 光武遣吳漢誅更始幽州牧苗曾，乃拜浮為大將軍幽州牧，守薊城，遂討定北邊。

translations.literal: Guangwu sent Wu Han to execute Gengshi's governor of Youzhou Miao Zeng, then appointed Fu as Grand General and governor of Youzhou, garrisoned Ji city, and thus pacified the northern frontier.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu dispatched Wu Han to put to death Miao Zeng, whom the Gengshi Emperor had placed over Youzhou; Zhu Fu was then named Grand General and governor of Youzhou, held Ji, and brought the northern border under control.

sentences.zh: 建武二年，封舞陽侯，食三縣。

translations.literal: In the second year of Jianwu he was enfeoffed as marquis of Wuyang with revenue from three counties.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 2 he was invested as Marquis of Wuyang with an income drawn from three counties.

sentences.zh: 浮年少有才能，頗欲厲風跡，收士心，辟召州中名宿涿郡王岑之屬，以為從事，及王莽時故吏二千石，皆引置幕府，乃多發諸郡倉谷，稟贍其妻子。

translations.literal: Fu was young and capable; he wished greatly to sharpen his reputation and win over the scholars, summoned eminent men of the province such as Wang Cen of Zhuojun for appointment as attendants, and former officials of two thousand piculs from Wang Mang's time—he brought them all into his headquarters—and issued much grain from the granaries of the various commanderies to feed their wives and children.
translations.idiomatic: Still young and talented, Zhu Fu sought to burnish his name and rally local elite support: he recruited notable figures such as Wang Cen of Zhuojun as staff officers and drew former Wang Mang–era commandery governors into his headquarters, then drew heavily on commandery granaries to provision their families.

sentences.zh: 漁陽太守彭寵以為天下未定，師旅方起，不宜多置官屬，以損軍實，不從其實。

translations.literal: The governor of Yuyang, Peng Chong, held that the empire was not yet settled and armies were only now rising; it was inappropriate to appoint many subordinates and thereby diminish military resources, and did not comply with this.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Chong, governor of Yuyang, argued that with the realm still unsettled and war only beginning, swelling the civil staff would waste military stores; he refused to go along.

sentences.zh: 浮性矜急自多，頗有不平，因以峻文詆，寵亦很強，歉負其功，嫌怨轉積。

translations.literal: Fu's nature was haughty, impatient, and self-important; he nursed considerable resentment and therefore attacked with harsh writings; Chong also was very stubborn, took offense at his own merit being slighted, and mutual dislike accumulated.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Fu was touchy, impatient, and full of himself, and smarted under the slight; he answered with biting prose. Peng Chong was equally unyielding and resentful that his contributions went unrecognized, and ill will deepened between them.

sentences.zh: 浮密奏寵遣吏迎妻而不迎其母，又受貨賄，殺害友人，多聚兵谷，意計難量。

translations.literal: Fu secretly memorialized that Chong had sent officials to welcome his wife but not his mother, had also taken bribes, killed a friend, and amassed much arms and grain—his intentions were hard to fathom.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Fu secretly reported that Peng Chong had officials fetch his wife but left his mother behind, accepted bribes, murdered a friend, and stockpiled troops and grain—conduct whose ultimate aim was anyone's guess.

sentences.zh: 寵既積怨，聞之，遂大怒，而舉兵攻浮。

translations.literal: Chong, having long nursed grievances, when he heard this became greatly enraged and raised troops to attack Fu.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Chong, already bitter, flew into a rage at the report and marched against Zhu Fu.

sentences.zh: 浮以書質責之曰：

translations.literal: Fu with a letter questioned and rebuked him, saying:
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Fu wrote him a letter of stern reproof:

sentences.zh: 蓋聞知者順時而謀，愚者逆理而動，常竊悲京城太叔以不知足而無賢輔，卒自棄於鄭也。

translations.literal: I have heard that the wise plan in accord with the times and the foolish act against the pattern; I have always privately grieved for the elder son of the capital, Taishu, who through discontent and lack of worthy assistance in the end cast himself away in Zheng.
translations.idiomatic: The wise read the times; the fool defies what is right. I have long pitied Prince Taishu of the Zheng capital—insatiable, without good counsel, and at last ruined in his own state.

sentences.zh: 伯通以名字典郡，有佐命之功，臨人親職，愛惜倉庫，而浮秉征伐之任，欲權時救急，二者皆為國耳。

translations.literal: Botong by reputation governs the commandery; he has merit in aiding the mandate, oversees the people in person, and cherishes the granaries; while Fu holds the charge of campaigning and wishes expediently to relieve urgent need—both are for the sake of the state.
translations.idiomatic: You, Botong, govern by renown and merit in founding the reign; you watch over the people yourself and husband the stores. I command the armies and bend rules to meet emergencies. Both of us serve the realm.

sentences.zh: 即疑浮相譖，何不詣闕自陳，而為族滅之計乎？

translations.literal: If you suspect Fu of slandering you, why not go to the palace gate and state your case yourself, and instead adopt a plan of exterminating your clan?
translations.idiomatic: If you think I have maligned you, present yourself at court and clear your name—why choose a course that ends in the extinction of your house?

sentences.zh: 朝廷之於伯通，恩亦厚矣，委以大郡，任以威武，事有柱石之寄，情同子孫之親。

translations.literal: The court toward Botong has also been generous: it has entrusted a great commandery to you and charged you with military authority; in affairs you are relied on as a pillar, in affection you are as close as children and grandchildren.
translations.idiomatic: The court has treated you generously—a major commandery, full military trust, the standing of a pillar of state, almost familial regard.

sentences.zh: 匹夫媵母尚能致命一餐，豈有身帶三綬，職典大邦，而不顧恩義，生心外畔者乎！

translations.literal: A common man or concubine mother can still sacrifice life for a single meal—how can one who wears three seals and holds office over a great state disregard grace and duty and harbor a heart of rebellion without?
translations.idiomatic: Common folk and serving women will die for a kindness once shown. Can a man who wears three ribbons of rank and governs a great region cast gratitude aside and turn rebel?

sentences.zh: 伯通與吏人語，何以為顏？

translations.literal: When Botong speaks with clerks and people, with what face?
translations.idiomatic: What face will you show your officials when you speak to them?

sentences.zh: 行步拜起，何以為容？

translations.literal: In walking, rising, bowing, what bearing?
translations.idiomatic: How will you carry yourself when you walk or rise to bow?

sentences.zh: 坐臥念之，何以為心？

translations.literal: Sitting and lying, pondering it, with what heart?
translations.idiomatic: When you sit or lie awake thinking of it, how will your conscience rest?

sentences.zh: 引鏡窺影，何施眉目？

translations.literal: Drawing the mirror to glimpse the reflection, how apply brow and eyes?
translations.idiomatic: When you lift the mirror, where will you put your eyes?

sentences.zh: 舉措建功，何以為人？

translations.literal: In undertaking affairs and establishing merit, how be a man?
translations.idiomatic: In whatever you do to win merit, what sort of man will you be?

sentences.zh: 惜乎棄休令之嘉名，造梟鴟之逆謀，捐傳世之慶祚，招破敗之重災，高論堯、舜之道，不忍桀、紂之性，生為世笑，死為愚鬼，不亦哀乎！

translations.literal: Alas, to cast away a fair and excellent name, devise an owl-and-kite treasonous plot, discard inherited blessing, summon crushing disaster, discourse loftily on the way of Yao and Shun, yet not curb the nature of Jie and Zhou—to live laughed at by the world and die a foolish ghost—is it not pitiful!
translations.idiomatic: What a waste—to trade a good name for treason fit for birds of prey, to throw away lasting fortune and court ruin, to preach Yao and Shun while indulging a temper worthy of Jie and Zhou: mocked in life, a fool in death—is that not tragedy?

sentences.zh: 伯通與耿俠遊俱起佐命，同被國恩。

translations.literal: Botong with Geng Xiayou together rose to aid the mandate and alike received the state's grace.
translations.idiomatic: You and Geng Yan both rose to help found the dynasty and share its favor.

sentences.zh: 俠遊廉讓，屢有降挹之言；

translations.literal: Xiayou was modest and yielding, repeatedly had words of self-lowering;
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan is humble and often speaks of his own unworthiness;

sentences.zh: 而伯通自伐，以為功高天下。

translations.literal: but Botong praises himself, thinking his merit highest under heaven.
translations.idiomatic: you boast that no one under heaven outranks your merit.

sentences.zh: 往時遼東有豕，生子白頭，異而獻之，行至河東，見群豕皆白，懷慚而還。

translations.literal: Formerly in Liaodong there was a pig that bore a white-headed piglet; thinking it strange they presented it; traveling to Hedong they saw the whole herd was white, and returned ashamed.
translations.idiomatic: There is the tale of the Liaodong pig whose white piglet seemed a marvel—until its owners reached Hedong and found every pig white, and slunk home in shame.

sentences.zh: 若以子之功論於朝廷，則為遼東豕也。

translations.literal: If your merit be weighed at court, it is the Liaodong pig.
translations.idiomatic: Measured at court, your exploits are that same Liaodong pig.

sentences.zh: 今乃愚妄，自比六國。

translations.literal: Now you are foolish and reckless, comparing yourself to the Six States.
translations.idiomatic: Now you play the fool and liken yourself to the Warring States.

sentences.zh: 六國之時，其勢各盛，廓土數千里，勝兵將百萬，故能據國相持，多歷年世。

translations.literal: In the time of the Six States each was strong in its power, territory stretched thousands of li, crack troops neared a million, hence they could hold states and face each other many years.
translations.idiomatic: In those days each power commanded thousands of li and nearly a million men—no wonder they could defy one another for generations.

sentences.zh: 今天下幾裏，列郡幾城，奈何以區區漁陽而結怨天子？

translations.literal: Now the realm has how many li, how many cities in the arrayed commanderies—how can you with mere Yuyang incur resentment from the Son of Heaven?
translations.idiomatic: Today the empire spans how many miles and commanderies—how can petty Yuyang afford the Son of Heaven's enmity?

sentences.zh: 此猶河濱之人捧土以塞孟津，多見其不知量也！

translations.literal: This is like a man by the riverbank scooping earth to block Meng Ford—mostly one sees he does not know his measure!
translations.idiomatic: You might as well scoop mud by the Yellow River to dam Meng Ford—proof you cannot gauge your own scale.

sentences.zh: 方今天下適定，海內願安，土無賢不肖，皆樂立名於世。

translations.literal: Just now the realm is becoming settled, within the seas they wish for peace; whether base or worthy, all joy in making a name in the age.
translations.idiomatic: The realm is just coming to rest; high and low alike want peace and a name that will last.

sentences.zh: 而伯通獨中風狂走，自捐盛時，內聽驕婦之失計，外信讒邪之諛言，長為群後惡法，永為功臣鑒戒，豈不誤哉！

translations.literal: But Botong alone, as if stricken and mad, casts away a flourishing time, within heeds a proud wife's bad plan, without believes slanderous flattery, will long be a vile model for the host of lords, forever a warning to meritorious subjects—how is it not error!
translations.idiomatic: You alone rage like a man in a fit, throwing away your prime: you heed a domineering wife within and flatterers without, and will stand forever as a warning to governors and generals—is that not folly?

sentences.zh: 定海內者無私仇，勿以前事自誤，願留意顧老母幼弟。

translations.literal: Those who pacify the seas have no private feuds; do not mislead yourself by former affairs; I wish you attend to your aged mother and younger brothers.
translations.idiomatic: Those who unite the realm nurse no private vendettas; do not let old scores undo you—think of your mother and your younger brothers.

sentences.zh: 凡舉事無為親厚者所痛，而為見仇者所快。

translations.literal: In all undertakings, do not be such that those who are intimate and close are pained, while those who are looked on as enemies rejoice.
translations.idiomatic: In whatever you do, do not grieve those who love you and delight those who wish you ill.

sentences.zh: 寵得書愈怒，攻浮轉急。

translations.literal: Chong when he received the letter grew angrier and attacked Fu with increasing urgency.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Chong only grew angrier at the letter and pressed the siege harder.

sentences.zh: 明年，涿郡太守張豐亦舉兵反。

translations.literal: The next year the governor of Zhuojun Zhang Feng also raised troops and rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: The following year Zhang Feng, governor of Zhuojun, rose in rebellion as well.

sentences.zh: 時，二郡畔戾，北州憂恐，浮以為天子必自將兵討之，而但遣遊擊將軍鄧隆陰助浮。

translations.literal: At that time the two commanderies were rebellious and depraved; the northern province was anxious and fearful; Fu thought the Son of Heaven would surely himself lead troops to punish them, but only sent Mobile General Deng Long secretly to aid Fu.
translations.idiomatic: With both commanderies in revolt, the north was terrified. Zhu Fu expected the emperor to take the field himself; instead only Mobile General Deng Long was sent to reinforce him covertly.

sentences.zh: 浮懷懼，以為帝怠於敵，不能救之，乃上疏曰：

translations.literal: Fu harbored fear, thinking the emperor was lax toward the enemy and could not rescue him, and presented a memorial saying:
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Fu grew afraid that the emperor was indifferent to the foe and would not save him, and submitted this memorial:

sentences.zh: 昔楚、宋列國，俱為諸侯，莊王以宋執其使，遂有投袂之師。

translations.literal: Formerly Chu and Song, arrayed states, were both feudal lords; King Zhuang of Chu because Song seized his envoy, thereupon had the army of casting aside the sleeve.
translations.idiomatic: When Chu and Song were coequal states, King Zhuang of Chu marched the moment Song seized his envoy—sleeve flung, army on the move.

sentences.zh: 魏公子顧朋友之要，觸冒強秦之鋒。

translations.literal: The lord of Wei remembered a friend's obligation and braved the keen edge of mighty Qin.
translations.idiomatic: Lord Xinling honored a friend's plea and threw himself against Qin's blades.

sentences.zh: 夫楚、魏非有分職匡正之大義也，莊王但為爭強而發忿，公子以一言而立信耳。

translations.literal: Now Chu and Wei did not have the great duty of divided offices and rectifying wrongs; King Zhuang merely for striving for dominance issued resentment; the lord by one word established trust.
translations.idiomatic: Neither Chu nor Wei had the solemn mandate of setting the realm right; King Zhuang acted from wounded pride, Xinling from a single pledge kept.

sentences.zh: 今彭寵反畔，張豐逆節，以為陛下必棄捐它事，以時滅之，既歷時月，寂寞無音。

translations.literal: Now Peng Chong rebels and turns traitor, Zhang Feng violates the norm; I thought Your Majesty would surely set aside other affairs and in due time destroy them; already months have passed, silent without sound.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Chong and Zhang Feng stand in open rebellion; we assumed Your Majesty would drop everything else and crush them promptly—yet months have dragged by in silence.

sentences.zh: 從圍城而不救，放逆虜而不討，臣誠惑之。

translations.literal: Following upon besieged cities yet not rescuing, releasing rebellious captives yet not punishing—your servant truly is perplexed.
translations.idiomatic: Our cities are besieged with no relief; rebels roam unpunished—I am at a loss to explain it.

sentences.zh: 昔高祖聖武，天下既定，猶身自征伐，未嘗寧居。

translations.literal: Formerly Gaozu was sacredly martial; though the realm was already settled, he still personally campaigned and never dwelt in tranquility.
translations.idiomatic: Even Gaozu, sage-warrior that he was, took the field himself once the empire was his—he never knew ease.

sentences.zh: 陛下雖興大業，海內未集，而獨逸豫，不顧北垂，百姓遑遑，無所系心。

translations.literal: Your Majesty though has raised great enterprise, within the seas not yet gathered, yet alone is leisurely and at ease, not attending to the northern border; the hundred surnames are restless, with nothing to tie their hearts.
translations.idiomatic: Your Majesty has restored the great enterprise while the realm is still unsettled, yet you linger at ease and ignore the northern frontier; the people drift anxious, with nothing to steady them.

sentences.zh: 三河、冀州，曷足以傳後哉！

translations.literal: The Three Rivers and Ji Province—how can they suffice to transmit to posterity!
translations.idiomatic: Will the Central Plain and Ji province alone be legacy enough for posterity?

sentences.zh: 今秋稼已熟，復為漁陽所掠。

translations.literal: Now autumn crops are already ripe, again they are plundered by Yuyang.
translations.idiomatic: The autumn harvest stands ready—and Yuyang strips it again.

sentences.zh: 張豐狂悖，奸黨日增，連年拒守，吏士疲勞，甲胄生蟣虱，弓弩不得施，上下焦心，相望救護，仰希陛下生活之恩。

translations.literal: Zhang Feng is wild and perverse; villainous factions daily increase; year after year they hold out on the defensive; clerks and soldiers are weary and exhausted; armor breeds lice and fleas; bows and crossbows cannot be deployed; high and low burn with anxiety, look to each other for rescue and protection, looking up in hope for Your Majesty's grace of granting life.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Feng grows bolder and his faction swells; year after year we endure siege until officers and men stagger under lice-ridden armor and idle crossbows. Every rank waits on tenterhooks for relief and Your Majesty's lifesaving grace.

sentences.zh: 詔報曰：『往年赤眉跋扈長安，吾策其無谷必東，果來歸降。

translations.literal: An edict replied saying: 'In former years the Red Eyebrows ran rampant in Chang'an; I calculated that without grain they must go east, and indeed they came and submitted.
translations.idiomatic: The edict answered: 'When the Red Eyebrows terrorized Chang'an, I reckoned they would lack grain and march east—and they came to surrender.'

sentences.zh: 今度此反虜，勢無久全，其中必有內相斬者。

translations.literal: Now I estimate these rebellious captives; in situation they cannot long remain whole; within them there must be those who will behead each other.
translations.idiomatic: These rebels cannot hold together long; I expect they will turn on one another.

sentences.zh: 今軍資未充，故須後麥耳。

translations.literal: Now military supplies are not yet sufficient; therefore we must wait for the later wheat.
translations.idiomatic: Our stores are still thin—we must wait for the late wheat.

sentences.zh: 』浮城中糧盡，人相食。

translations.literal: ' In Fu's city grain was exhausted; people ate each other.
translations.idiomatic: ' Meanwhile grain in Zhu Fu's city ran out and the people turned to cannibalism.

sentences.zh: 會上谷太守耿況遣騎來救浮，浮乃得遁走。

translations.literal: It happened that the governor of Shanggu Geng Kuang sent cavalry to rescue Fu, and Fu was then able to flee.
translations.idiomatic: Then Geng Kuang, governor of Shanggu, sent horsemen to Zhu Fu's relief, and Zhu Fu broke out and escaped.

sentences.zh: 南至良鄉，其兵長反遮之，浮恐不得脫，乃下馬刺殺其妻，僅以身免，城降於寵，尚書令侯霸奏浮敗亂幽州，構成寵罪，徒勞軍師，不能死節，罪當伏誅。

translations.literal: Southward to Liangxiang, his troop chief turned and blocked him; Fu feared he could not escape, then dismounted and stabbed his wife to death, barely escaping with his body alone; the city surrendered to Chong; Minister Secretary Hou Ba memorialized that Fu had defeated and thrown Youzhou into chaos, fabricated Chong's crimes, vainly toiled the army, could not die for principle—crime deserved execution.
translations.idiomatic: South of Liangxiang his own officers turned on him. Fearing capture, Zhu Fu dismounted and killed his wife to flee alone. The city fell to Peng Chong. Minister Secretary Hou Ba accused him of ruining Youzhou, inventing charges against Peng Chong, wasting the army, and failing to die with honor—all capital offenses.

sentences.zh: 帝不忍，以浮代賈復為執金吾，徙封父城侯。

translations.literal: The emperor could not bear it; he replaced Jia Fu with Fu as Bearer of the Mace and transferred his fief to marquis of Fucheng.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor spared him: Zhu Fu succeeded Jia Fu as Bearer of the Golden Mace and was transferred to the marquisate of Fucheng.

sentences.zh: 後豐、寵並自敗。

translations.literal: Later Feng and Chong alike perished of themselves.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Feng and Peng Chong were later destroyed in turn.

sentences.zh: 帝以二千石長吏多不勝任，時有纖微之過者，必見斥罷，交易紛擾，百姓不寧。

translations.literal: The emperor because governors of two thousand piculs often did not meet appointment, when there were slight faults they were invariably seen dismissed and replaced; exchange and turnover was confused and disturbing; the hundred surnames were not tranquil.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor found too many commandery governors wanting: the slightest fault meant dismissal and replacement, endless churn in office, and unrest among the people.

sentences.zh: 六年，有日食之異，浮因上疏曰：

translations.literal: In the sixth year there was the anomaly of a solar eclipse; Fu thereupon presented a memorial saying:
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year a solar eclipse occurred, and Zhu Fu submitted a memorial:

sentences.zh: 臣聞日者眾陽之所宗，君上之位也。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard that the sun is what the multitude of yangs honor; it is the position of the lord above.
translations.idiomatic: I have read that the sun is sovereign among the yang forces and emblematic of the ruler.

sentences.zh: 凡居官治民，據郡典縣，皆為陽為上，為尊為長。

translations.literal: All who occupy office and govern the people, who hold commanderies and oversee counties, are all yang, are above, are honored, are elder.
translations.idiomatic: Every official who governs people—whether over a commandery or a county—stands for the yang, the superior, the honored head.

sentences.zh: 若陽上不明，尊長不足，則幹動三光，垂示王者。

translations.literal: If the yang above is not bright, if respect for elders is insufficient, then it perturbs the Three Lights and hangs a sign for the king.
translations.idiomatic: When those who should illuminate falter and authority slackens, sun, moon, and stars are shaken—a warning to the throne.

sentences.zh: 五典紀國家之政，《鴻範》別災異之文，皆宣明天道，以征來事者也。

translations.literal: The Five Canons record the state's governance; the 'Hong Fan' distinguishes the texts of disasters and prodigies—all make clear the way of heaven, to verify coming affairs.
translations.idiomatic: The Five Canons record statecraft; the Hong Fan sorts portents—all expound Heaven's pattern so we may read what follows.

sentences.zh: 陛下哀湣海內新罹禍毒，保宥生人，使得蘇息。

translations.literal: Your Majesty pities within the seas those newly afflicted by calamity and poison, protects and forgives the living, causing them to obtain relief and rest.
translations.idiomatic: Your Majesty has pitied a people newly scourged by war, sheltered the living, and let them breathe again.

sentences.zh: 而今牧人之吏，多未稱職，小違理實，輒見斥罷，豈不粲然黑白分明哉！

translations.literal: But now shepherds of the people, many do not match office; small deviation from pattern and substance, immediately they are seen dismissed—is it not dazzlingly black and white distinct!
translations.idiomatic: Yet the men who shepherd the people are judged harshly: stray slightly from the rule and they are cashiered—does justice need to be that ruthlessly plain?

sentences.zh: 然以堯、舜之盛，猶如三考，大漢之興，亦累功效，吏皆積久，養老於官，至名子孫，因為氏姓。

translations.literal: Yet by the flourishing of Yao and Shun, still there were three examinations; the rise of Great Han also accumulated merit and effect; clerks all accumulated long, aged in office, reached naming children and grandchildren, thereby became clan surnames.
translations.idiomatic: Even under Yao and Shun, officers sat three rounds of review; our own dynasty rose by steady service—officials grew old in post, passed offices to sons, and surnames took root.

sentences.zh: 當時吏職，何能悉理；

translations.literal: At that time clerical duties—how could they all be managed;
translations.idiomatic: No age has ever run government flawlessly;

sentences.zh: 論議之徒，豈不喧嘩。

translations.literal: the tribe of discussers and debaters—how could they not clamor.
translations.idiomatic: grumblers have always made noise.

sentences.zh: 蓋以為天地之功不可倉卒，艱難之業當累日也。

translations.literal: For it is thought that the merit of heaven and earth cannot be hasty; the enterprise of hardship and difficulty ought to accumulate days.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's work is not done in haste; hard-won order needs years to mature.

sentences.zh: 而間者守宰數見換易，迎新相代，疲勞道路。

translations.literal: But recently governors and shepherds have repeatedly been seen replaced and changed; welcoming the new, succeeding each other, weary and exhausted on the roads.
translations.idiomatic: Lately prefects and magistrates are swapped so often that officers race the roads to take up new posts until they drop.

sentences.zh: 尋其視事日淺，未足昭見其職，既加嚴切，人不自呆，各自顧望，無自安之心。

translations.literal: Trace them: their days in attending to affairs are shallow, not sufficient to clearly manifest their office; already applying severity and keenness, people do not protect themselves, each looks about in hesitation, without a heart of self-tranquility.
translations.idiomatic: They barely warm the seat before harsh scrutiny falls; men cannot protect themselves, glance sideways, and never feel secure.

sentences.zh: 有司或因睚眥以騁私怨，茍求長短，求媚上意。

translations.literal: Responsible offices perhaps because of a sidelong glance give rein to private resentment; carelessly seek faults and lengths; seek to flatter the superior's intent.
translations.idiomatic: Petty officials nurse grudges over trifles, fish for fault, and curry favor upstairs.

sentences.zh: 二千石及長吏迫於舉劾，懼於刺譏，故爭飾詐偽，以希虛譽。

translations.literal: Those of two thousand piculs and senior clerks pressed by impeachment, fearful of stab-and-ridicule, therefore strive to adorn deceit and pretense, in hope of empty reputation.
translations.idiomatic: Commandery heads and their staffs, hounded by impeachment and backbiting, compete in hollow posturing for empty praise.

sentences.zh: 斯皆群陽騷動，日月失行之應。

translations.literal: These are all the throng of yangs disturbed, the corresponding sign of sun and moon losing their courses.
translations.idiomatic: All this is yang in turmoil—the sun and moon answer with disorder.

sentences.zh: 夫物暴長者必矢折，功卒成者必亟壞，如摧長久之業，而造速成之功，非陛下之福也。

translations.literal: Now things that burst in growing must snap like an arrow; merit that finishes in haste must swiftly break; like destroying long-lasting enterprise and making finished-in-haste merit—it is not Your Majesty's blessing.
translations.idiomatic: What shoots up fast snaps; what is rushed to completion cracks. To wreck lasting rule for a quick fix does Your Majesty no good.

sentences.zh: 天下非一時之用也，海內非一旦之功也。

translations.literal: The realm is not for one moment's use; within the seas is not one dawn's merit.
translations.idiomatic: The empire is not built in a day, nor the realm won in a morning.

sentences.zh: 願陛下遊意於經年之外，望化於一世之後，天下幸甚。

translations.literal: I wish Your Majesty ramble your intent beyond spanning years, hope for transformation after one generation—the realm will be greatly fortunate.
translations.idiomatic: May Your Majesty look beyond the next inspection cycle and seek transformation across a generation—the empire would be the richer.

sentences.zh: 帝下其議，群臣多同於浮，自是牧守易代頗簡。

translations.literal: The emperor sent down his deliberation; the host of ministers mostly agreed with Fu; from this replacement of shepherds and governors was somewhat reduced.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor referred the memorial to court; most ministers sided with Zhu Fu, and thereafter turnover among governors grew less frenetic.

sentences.zh: 七年，轉太仆。

translations.literal: In the seventh year he was transferred to Grand Coachman.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh year he was moved to Grand Coachman.

sentences.zh: 浮又以國學既興，宜廣博士之選，乃上書曰：

translations.literal: Fu again because the national school had already risen held that the selection of erudites should be broadened, and presented a letter saying:
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Fu argued that with the Imperial Academy now founded the pool of erudites should be widened, and wrote:

sentences.zh: 夫太學者，禮義之官，教化所由興也。

translations.literal: Now the Grand Learning is the office of ritual and rightness; it is whence transformation by teaching arises.
translations.idiomatic: The Imperial Academy is the seat of ritual and moral instruction—where civilizing teaching begins.

sentences.zh: 陛下尊敬先聖，垂意古典，官室未飾，幹戈未休，而先建太學，進立橫舍，比日車駕親臨觀饗，將以弘時雍之化，顯勉進之功也。

translations.literal: Your Majesty honors former sages, hangs intent on classical antiquity; palace chambers not yet adorned, shields and spears not yet ceased, yet first built Grand Learning, advanced and established the lateral lodges; in recent days the imperial carriage has personally come to view the feast—it is thereby to enlarge the transformation of timely harmony, display the merit of encouragement and advance.
translations.idiomatic: You honor the ancient sages and cherish the classics: though palaces remain unfinished and arms still clash, you founded the Academy and raised its halls; of late you have attended the sacrifice in person—to spread harmonious rule and hold up the ideal of learning.

sentences.zh: 尋博士之官，為天下宗師，使孔聖之言傳而不絕。

translations.literal: Trace the office of erudite: it is model teacher to the realm, causing the words of the sage Confucius to transmit and not be cut off.
translations.idiomatic: The erudites are teachers to the empire; through them the words of Confucius endure.

sentences.zh: 舊事，策試博士，必廣求詳選，爰自畿夏，延及四方，是以博舉明經，惟賢是登，學者精勵，遠近同慕，伏聞詔書更試五人，惟取見在洛陽城者。

translations.literal: By old practice, examining erudites by policy questions, one must broadly seek and carefully select, from the capital region extending to the four directions; hence broadly raising those clear in the classics, only the worthy were elevated; scholars were refined and diligent, far and near alike admired. I humbly hear the edict script again examines five men, only taking those presently within Luoyang city.
translations.idiomatic: Formerly, doctoral examinations drew widely from capital and provinces alike—only the worthy versed in the classics advanced, and scholars everywhere aspired to compete. I hear the latest edict retests five candidates restricted to those already inside Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 臣恐自今以往，將有所失。

translations.literal: Your servant fears from now onward there will be loss.
translations.idiomatic: I fear we will lose something vital if this continues.

sentences.zh: 求之密邇，容或未盡，而四方之學，無所勸樂。

translations.literal: Seeking them close nearby, perhaps there are not yet exhausted, but learning of the four directions has nothing by which to be encouraged and delighted.
translations.idiomatic: Searching only nearby may miss talent, while students across the realm lose heart.

sentences.zh: 凡策試之本，貴得其真，非有期會，不及遠方也。

translations.literal: In all policy examination's root, it values obtaining their truth; it is not that there is a scheduled assembly and does not reach distant regions.
translations.idiomatic: The point of the examination is genuine merit, not a fixed roster that shuts out the distant.

sentences.zh: 又諸所征試，皆私自發遣，非有傷費煩擾於事也。

translations.literal: Moreover all those summoned for examination are privately sent off on their own; it is not that there is harm to expense or vexation to affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Candidates travel at their own expense—no burden on the treasury or needless fuss.

sentences.zh: 語曰：『中國失禮，求之於野。

translations.literal: A saying says: 'When the Central States lose ritual, seek it in the wilds.
translations.idiomatic: The proverb runs: 'When the heartland lacks ritual, look to the hinterland.'

sentences.zh: 』臣浮幸得與講圖讖，故敢越職。

translations.literal: ' Your servant Fu fortunately obtained participation in expounding charts and prophecies, therefore dares exceed office.
translations.idiomatic: ' I have been privileged to discuss the prognostic texts—hence this breach of protocol.

sentences.zh: 帝然之。

translations.literal: The emperor approved it.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor agreed.

sentences.zh: 二十年，代竇融為大司空，二十二年，坐賣弄國恩免。

translations.literal: In the twentieth year he replaced Dou Rong as Grand Minister of Works; in the twenty-second year he sat for trafficking and displaying state grace and was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: In year 20 he succeeded Dou Rong as Grand Minister of Works; in year 22 he was removed for abusing imperial favor.

sentences.zh: 二十五年，徙封新息侯。

translations.literal: In the twenty-fifth year his fief was transferred to marquis of Xinxi.
translations.idiomatic: In year 25 his title was moved to Marquis of Xinxi.

sentences.zh: 帝以浮陵轢同列，每銜之，惜其功能，不忍加罪。

translations.literal: The emperor because Fu rode roughshod over his peers, often harbored resentment against him, yet cherished his merit and ability, could not bear to add crime.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Fu rode roughshod over colleagues; the emperor resented it yet valued his abilities too much to punish him.

sentences.zh: 永平中，有人單辭告浮事者，顯宗大怒，賜浮死。

translations.literal: In the Yongping period someone with solitary words denounced Fu's affairs; Emperor Xiaozong was greatly angered and granted Fu death.
translations.idiomatic: During Yongping a lone accuser denounced Zhu Fu; Emperor Ming flew into a rage and ordered him to die.

sentences.zh: 長水校尉樊鯈言於帝曰：『唐堯大聖，兆人獲所，尚優遊四兇之獄，厭服海內之心，使天下鹹知，然後殛罰。

translations.literal: Colonel of Changshui Fan Xiu spoke to the emperor, saying: 'Tang Yao was greatly sage; the myriad people obtained their place; still he dealt deliberately with the Four Villains, satisfied and subdued the hearts within the seas, caused all under heaven alike to know, then afterward applied execution and punishment.
translations.idiomatic: Colonel of Changshui Fan Xiu urged the throne: 'Even sage Yao, once the people had their places, moved deliberately against the Four Villains, won the empire's trust, and let all understand before he punished.

sentences.zh: 浮事雖昭明，而未達人聽，宜下廷尉，章著其事。

translations.literal: Fu's affairs though are clear and bright, yet have not reached people's hearing; it is fitting to send down to the commandant of justice, make manifest his affairs in writing.'
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Fu's case may be clear, but it has not reached public hearing—refer it to the commandant of justice and publish the facts.'

sentences.zh: 』帝亦悔之。

translations.literal: ' The emperor also regretted it.
translations.idiomatic: ' The emperor repented of his haste.

sentences.zh: 論曰：吳起與田文論功，文不及者三，朱買臣難公孫弘十策，弘不得其一，終之田文相魏，公孫宰漢，誠知宰相自有體也。

translations.literal: The appraisal says: Wu Qi debated merit with Tian Wen; Wen did not reach him in three respects; Zhu Maichen challenged Gongsun Hong with ten strategies; Hong did not obtain one of them—in the end Tian Wen ministered Wei, Gongsun ruled Han; truly they knew the prime minister has his own substance.
translations.idiomatic: The historian remarks: Wu Qi disputed merit with Tian Wen and surpassed him in three ways; Zhu Maichen posed ten challenges to Gongsun Hong that Hong could not meet—yet Tian Wen became chief minister of Wei and Gongsun governed Han. Chancellorship is a different art altogether.

sentences.zh: 故曾子曰：『君子所貴乎道者三，籩豆之事則有司存。

translations.literal: Therefore Master Zeng said: 'What the noble man prizes in the Way are three; matters of baskets and stands then there are officers who preserve.
translations.idiomatic: As Zengzi said, 'The gentleman honors three things in the Way; the plates and dishes are for petty officers to mind.'

sentences.zh: 』而光武、明帝躬好吏事，亦以課核三公，其人或失而其禮稍薄，至有誅斥詰辱之累。

translations.literal: ' Yet Guangwu and Emperor Ming personally favored clerical affairs, also used examination and verification on the Three Dukes; those persons sometimes erred and their ritual grew somewhat thin, reaching to the accumulation of execution-rejection, interrogation-insult.
translations.idiomatic: ' Yet Guangwu and Ming loved paperwork and audited the Three Councillors themselves; ministers lost dignity, ritual thinned, and some ended in execution, dismissal, or public humiliation.

sentences.zh: 任職責過，一至於此，追感賈生之論，不亦篤乎！

translations.literal: Holding office and blaming faults—it has reached this extent; tracing back and pondering Jia Yi's discourse—is it not profound!
translations.idiomatic: To hold office yet fault-find to this degree calls Jia Yi's essay to mind—and shows how grave the matter is!

sentences.zh: 朱浮譏諷苛察欲速之弊，然矣，焉得長者之言哉！

translations.literal: Zhu Fu ridiculed and satirized the harms of harsh scrutiny and desiring haste—true indeed; how could one obtain the words of elders!
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Fu mocked the zeal for harsh scrutiny and quick fixes—and he was right; where was the steady counsel of a seasoned elder?

sentences.zh: 馮魴字孝孫，南陽湖陽人也。

translations.literal: Feng Fang, courtesy name Xiaosun, was a man of Huyang in Nanyang.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Fang, styled Xiaosun, came from Huyang in Nanyang commandery.

sentences.zh: 其先魏之支別，食菜馮城，因以氏焉。

translations.literal: His ancestors were a collateral branch of Wei; they ate vegetables at Feng city and thereby took it as surname.
translations.idiomatic: His line branched from Wei and held the fief of Feng—hence the surname.

sentences.zh: 秦滅魏，遷於湖陽，為郡族姓。

translations.literal: Qin destroyed Wei; they moved to Huyang and became a great clan surname of the commandery.
translations.idiomatic: When Qin conquered Wei, the family moved to Huyang and grew into a leading local clan.

sentences.zh: 王莽末，四方潰畔，魴乃聚賓客，招豪桀，作營塹，以待所歸。

translations.literal: At the end of Wang Mang, the four quarters collapsed in rebellion; Fang then gathered retainers, summoned heroes, built ramparts and ditches, and awaited whom they would attach to.
translations.idiomatic: When Wang Mang fell and chaos spread, Feng Fang gathered clients and bold spirits, threw up fortified camps, and waited to see which banner would prevail.

sentences.zh: 是時湖陽大姓虞都尉反城稱兵，先與同縣申屠季有仇，而殺其兄，謀滅季族。

translations.literal: At this time the magnate of Huyang Captain Yu rebelled in the city and raised arms; previously he had enmity with Shentu Ji of the same county and killed his elder brother, plotting to exterminate Ji's clan.
translations.idiomatic: A local magnate styled Captain Yu seized Huyang and raised troops; he had long feuded with Shentu Ji—having murdered Ji's elder brother, he now meant to wipe out the whole clan.

sentences.zh: 季亡歸魴，魴將季欲還其宮，道逢都尉從弟長卿來，欲執季。

translations.literal: Ji fled and returned to Fang; Fang was about to lead Ji back to his residence when on the road they met the captain's cousin Changqing coming, wishing to seize Ji.
translations.idiomatic: Shentu Ji fled to Feng Fang, who escorted him toward home until they met Captain Yu's cousin Changqing, who moved to arrest Ji.

sentences.zh: 魴叱長卿曰：『我與季雖無素故，士窮想歸，要當以死任之，卿為何言？

translations.literal: Fang rebuked Changqing, saying: 'Though Ji and I lack former acquaintance, when a gentleman is destitute he wishes to return—I must answer that with my death—what words are yours?
translations.idiomatic: Feng Fang shouted down Changqing: 'Ji and I were strangers until today, but a man hard pressed seeks shelter—I will answer for him with my life. What business is it of yours?'

sentences.zh: 』遂與俱歸。

translations.literal: ' Then together they returned.
translations.idiomatic: ' They went home together.

sentences.zh: 季謝曰：『蒙恩得全，死無以為報，有牛馬財物，願悉獻之。

translations.literal: Ji thanked saying: 'Receiving grace I have obtained preservation; in death I have nothing wherewith to repay; I have oxen, horses, and goods—I wish to present them all.
translations.idiomatic: Shentu Ji said, 'You spared my life; death could not repay you—take my herds and goods.'

sentences.zh: 』魴作色曰：『吾老親弱弟皆在賊城中，今日相與，尚無所顧，何雲財物乎？

translations.literal: ' Fang changed color and said: 'My aged parents and younger brothers are all in the rebel city; today we associate together—I still attend to nothing—what speak of goods?
translations.idiomatic: ' Feng Fang flushed and replied, 'My parents and young brothers are still trapped in that rebel town—together we risk everything; why speak of property?'

sentences.zh: 』季慚不敢復言。

translations.literal: ' Ji was ashamed and did not dare speak again.
translations.idiomatic: ' Shentu Ji fell silent for shame.

sentences.zh: 魴自是為縣邑所敬信，故能據營自固。

translations.literal: From this Fang was honored and trusted by the county and therefore was able to hold the camp and secure himself.
translations.idiomatic: From then on the county looked to Feng Fang with respect; his camp held firm.

sentences.zh: 時天下未定，而四方之士擁兵矯稱者甚眾，唯魴自守，兼有方略。

translations.literal: At the time the realm was not yet settled, yet gentlemen of the four quarters who grasped arms and falsely styled themselves were very many; only Fang guarded himself and moreover had strategy and method.
translations.idiomatic: While the realm still churned, adventurers everywhere seized titles and troops; Feng Fang alone held his ground with steady judgment.

sentences.zh: 光武聞而嘉之，建武三年，征詣行在所，見於雲臺，拜虞令。

translations.literal: Guangwu heard and praised him; in the third year of Jianwu summoned him to the traveling headquarters, saw him at Cloud Terrace, appointed magistrate of Yu.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu approved and in Jianwu 3 summoned him to court, received him at the Cloud Terrace, and named him magistrate of Yu.

sentences.zh: 為政敢殺伐，以威信稱。

translations.literal: In governing he dared killing and striking; he was acclaimed by awe-inspiring trust.
translations.idiomatic: He governed boldly and struck fear; men respected his authority.

sentences.zh: 遷郟令。

translations.literal: He was transferred to magistrate of Jia.
translations.idiomatic: He was promoted to magistrate of Jia.

sentences.zh: 後車駕西征隗囂，潁川資賊群起，郟賊延褒等眾三千餘人，攻圍縣舍，魴率吏士七十許人，力戰連日，弩矢盡，城陷，魴乃遁去。

translations.literal: Later the imperial carriage campaigned west against Wei Xiao; in Yingchuan bandits rose in groups; the Jia bandits Yan Bao and others, a crowd of more than three thousand, attacked and besieged the county headquarters; Fang led clerks and soldiers about seventy men, fought hard successive days, crossbow bolts exhausted, the city fell, Fang then fled.
translations.idiomatic: During the western campaign against Wei Xiao, Yingchuan erupted in raids; over three thousand rebels led by Yan Bao of Jia besieged the county seat. Feng Fang led seventy-odd men, fought until his bolts ran out, lost the town, and withdrew.

sentences.zh: 帝聞郡國反，即馳赴潁川，魴詣行在所。

translations.literal: The emperor heard commanderies and kingdoms rebelled, immediately galloped to Yingchuan; Fang went to the traveling headquarters.
translations.idiomatic: Learning of revolts across the commanderies, the emperor raced to Yingchuan; Feng Fang presented himself at court.

sentences.zh: 帝案行鬥處，知魴力戰，乃嘉之曰：『此健令也。

translations.literal: The emperor inspected and traveled the battle place, knew Fang had fought hard, then praised saying: 'This is a stalwart magistrate.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor toured the battlefield, saw how hard Feng Fang had fought, and said, 'There is a magistrate worth his salt.'

sentences.zh: 所當討擊，勿拘州郡。

translations.literal: Where ought to be punished and struck, do not be constrained by commandery and county.
translations.idiomatic: Strike wherever rebels appear—do not let administrative lines stop you.

sentences.zh: 』褒等聞帝至，皆自髡剔，負鈇锧，將其眾請罪。

translations.literal: ' When Bao and others heard the emperor had arrived, all shaved themselves and tattooed, bore axe and chopping-block, led their hosts and asked guilt.
translations.idiomatic: ' When Yan Bao's men learned the emperor had come, they shaved their heads, loaded executioner's blocks onto their backs, and brought their followers to surrender.

sentences.zh: 帝且赦之，使魴轉降諸聚落，縣中平定，詔乃悉以褒等還魴誅之。

translations.literal: The emperor temporarily pardoned them, caused Fang to turn and surrender the various settlements; within the county was pacified; an edict then entirely gave Bao and others back to Fang to execute them.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor stayed execution long enough for Feng Fang to round up the rebel camps; once the county was quiet, he handed Yan Bao and the rest back to Feng Fang for punishment.

sentences.zh: 魴責讓以行軍法，皆叩頭曰：『今日受誅，死無所恨。

translations.literal: Fang reproached and blamed them by carrying out military law; all knocked heads saying: 'Today receiving execution, in death we have no resentment.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Fang rebuked them under military law; they kowtowed and said, 'If we die today, we have no complaint.'

sentences.zh: 』魴曰：『汝知悔過伏罪，今一切相赦，聽各反農桑，為令作耳目。

translations.literal: ' Fang said: 'You know repentance and submitting guilt; now entirely mutually pardon you; hear each return to farming and mulberry; act as eyes and ears for the magistrate.
translations.idiomatic: ' Feng Fang answered, 'You admit fault—then I pardon you all: go back to your fields and orchards and serve the county as my eyes and ears.'

sentences.zh: 』皆稱萬歲。

translations.literal: ' All cried ten thousand years.
translations.idiomatic: ' They shouted long live the throne.

sentences.zh: 是時每有盜賊，並為褒等所發，無敢動者，縣界清靜。

translations.literal: At this time whenever there were robbers and bandits, together they were exposed by Bao and others; none dared move; the county borders were clear and quiet.
translations.idiomatic: Thereafter every theft was reported through Yan Bao's network; no one dared stir, and the county stayed calm.

sentences.zh: 魴性矜嚴公正，在位數進忠言，多見納用。

translations.literal: Fang's nature was stern and just; in office he repeatedly advanced loyal words and many were accepted and employed.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Fang was austere and fair; his blunt counsel from office was often taken.

sentences.zh: 十四年，詔復爵士。

translations.literal: In the fourteenth year an edict restored noble rank.
translations.idiomatic: In year 14 an edict restored his noble title.

sentences.zh: 明年，東巡郡國，留魴宿衛南宮。

translations.literal: The next year he toured east commanderies and kingdoms, left Fang to lodge guard Southern Palace.
translations.idiomatic: The next year the emperor toured the east and left Feng Fang to stand night guard at the Southern Palace.

sentences.zh: 建初三年，以老病乞身，肅宗許之。

translations.literal: In the third year of Jianchu, because of old age and illness begged his person; Emperor Suzong granted it.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianchu 3 he retired on grounds of age and illness; Emperor Zhang approved.

sentences.zh: 其冬為五更，詔魴朝賀，就列侯位。

translations.literal: That winter he became one of the Five Elders; an edict ordered Fang to court congratulation, taking the marquis's place in rank.
translations.idiomatic: That winter he was named to the Five Elders and summoned to New Year's audience with the rank of a full marquis.

sentences.zh: 元和二年，卒，時年八十六。

translations.literal: In the second year of Yuanhe he died, aged eighty-six.
translations.idiomatic: He died in Yuanhe 2 at the age of eighty-six.

sentences.zh: 子柱嗣。

translations.literal: His son Zhu succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: His son Feng Zhu inherited the title.

sentences.zh: 尚顯宗女獲嘉長公主，少為侍中，以恭肅謙約稱，位至將作大匠。

translations.literal: He married Emperor Xiaozong's daughter the senior princess of Huojia; in youth was attendant at the palace gates; by respectful solemnity and humble restraint was acclaimed; rank reached superintendent of works.
translations.idiomatic: He married Emperor Ming's daughter, the Princess of Huojia, served young as palace attendant, won praise for modest dignity, and rose to superintendent of public works.

sentences.zh: 柱卒，子定嗣，官至羽林中郎將。

translations.literal: When Zhu died, his son Ding succeeded; office reached colonel of the Feathered Forest.
translations.idiomatic: When Feng Zhu died, his son Feng Ding inherited and rose to colonel of the Feathered Forest guard.

sentences.zh: 定卒，無子，國除。

translations.literal: When Ding died, without son, the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Ding left no son and the marquisate lapsed.

sentences.zh: 定弟石，襲母公主封獲嘉侯，亦為侍中，稍遷衛尉。

translations.literal: Ding's younger brother Shi, inherited mother's princess fief as marquis of Huojia; also was attendant at the palace gates; gradually transferred to minister of the guards.
translations.idiomatic: His younger brother Feng Shi inherited the Princess of Huojia's marquisate of Huojia, served as palace attendant, and advanced to minister of the guards.

sentences.zh: 能取悅當世，為安帝所寵。

translations.literal: Able to please his generation; was favored by Emperor An.
translations.idiomatic: He knew how to please the court and became a favorite of Emperor An.

sentences.zh: 帝嘗幸其府，留飲十許日，賜駁犀具劍、佩刀、紫艾綬、玉玦各一，拜子世為黃門侍郎，世弟二人皆郎中。

translations.literal: The emperor once favored his residence, stayed drinking about ten days, granted a dappled-rhino-hilt sword, belt knife, purple mugwort ribbon, and jade ring, one of each; appointed his son Shi as gentleman of the Yellow Gates; two younger brothers of Shi were all consultants (langzhong).
translations.idiomatic: The emperor once lodged at his mansion for some ten days of feasting and gave him a rhinoceros-horn hilt sword, belt knife, purple grandee's ribbon, and jade ring. He appointed Feng Shi's son Shi as gentleman of the Yellow Gates and named both of Shi's younger brothers court gentlemen.

sentences.zh: 自永初兵荒，王侯租秩多不充，於是特詔以它縣租稅足石，今如舊限，歲入谷三萬斛，錢四萬。

translations.literal: From Yongchu military famine, marquises' rent and stipends mostly did not suffice; then a special edict used other counties' rent and tax to complete Shi's quota; now like old limit, yearly income grain thirty thousand hu, cash forty thousand.
translations.idiomatic: After the Yongchu wars many marquises could not collect their stipends; an edict topped up Feng Shi's income from other counties' taxes—restoring the old quota of thirty thousand hu of grain and forty thousand cash a year.

sentences.zh: 遷光祿勛，遂代楊震為太尉。

translations.literal: He was transferred to superintendent of the palace guards, then replaced Yang Zhen as grand commandant.
translations.idiomatic: He rose to superintendent of the palace guards and succeeded Yang Zhen as grand commandant.

sentences.zh: 及北鄉侯立，遷太傅，與太尉東萊劉喜參錄尚書事。

translations.literal: When the marquis of Beixiang was established, he was transferred to grand tutor, with grand commandant Liu Xi of Donglai jointly recording Masters of Writing affairs.
translations.idiomatic: When the Marquis of Beixiang acceded, Feng Shi became grand tutor and, with Grand Commandant Liu Xi of Donglai, shared supervision of the Masters of Writing.

sentences.zh: 順帝既立，石與喜皆以阿黨閻顯、江京等策免，復為衛尉。

translations.literal: When Emperor Shun was already established, Shi and Xi both because of currying faction with Yan Xian, Jiang Jing and others were removed by edict; again became minister of the guards.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Shun took the throne, Feng Shi and Liu Xi were cashiered for colluding with Yan Xian and Jiang Jing; Feng Shi later returned as minister of the guards.

sentences.zh: 卒，子代嗣。

translations.literal: He died; his son Dai succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: He died and his son Feng Dai inherited.

sentences.zh: 代卒，弟承嗣，為步兵校尉。

translations.literal: When Dai died, younger brother Cheng succeeded; became colonel of foot soldiers.
translations.idiomatic: When Feng Dai died, his brother Feng Cheng inherited and served as colonel of infantry.

sentences.zh: 石弟光，和帝時詔封楊邑侯，亦以石寵，官至城門校尉。

translations.literal: Shi's younger brother Guang, in Emperor He's time edict enfeoffed marquis of Yangyi; also because of Shi's favor; office reached colonel of the city gates.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Guang, Feng Shi's younger brother, was enfeoffed as Marquis of Yangyi under Emperor He through his brother's influence and rose to colonel of the city gates.

sentences.zh: 卒，子肅嗣，為黃門侍郎。

translations.literal: He died; his son Su succeeded; became gentleman of the Yellow Gates.
translations.idiomatic: He died and was succeeded by his son Feng Su as gentleman of the Yellow Gates.

sentences.zh: 虞延字子大，陳留東昏人也。

translations.literal: Yu Yan, courtesy name Zida, was a man of Donghun in Chenliu.
translations.idiomatic: Yu Yan, styled Zida, came from Donghun in Chenliu commandery.

sentences.zh: 延初生，其上有物若一匹練，遂上升上，占者以為吉。

translations.literal: When Yan was first born, above him there was a thing like one bolt of white silk; then it ascended upward; diviners held it auspicious.
translations.idiomatic: At Yu Yan's birth something like a bolt of white silk hovered overhead and drifted upward—diviners took it for a good omen.

sentences.zh: 及長，長八尺六寸，要帶十圍，力能扛鼎。

translations.literal: When grown, height eight chi six cun; waist belt ten girths; strength able to shoulder a tripod.
translations.idiomatic: Grown, he stood eight chi six cun tall, his girth measured ten spans round the waist, and he could hoist a bronze tripod.

sentences.zh: 少為戶牖亭長。

translations.literal: In youth he was chief of Huyou pavilion.
translations.idiomatic: In his youth he was chief of Huyou pavilion.

sentences.zh: 時王莽貴人魏氏賓客放從，延率吏卒突入其家捕之，以此見怨，故位不升。

translations.literal: At the time Wang Mang's honored lady Wei's retainers ran riot; Yan led clerks and soldiers burst into their house and seized them; because of this was resented, therefore rank did not rise.
translations.idiomatic: When favorites of Wang Mang's Wei consort ran riot, Yu Yan stormed their house with his men and made arrests—earning enemies that stalled his career.

sentences.zh: 性敦樸，不拘小節，又無鄉曲之譽。

translations.literal: His nature was honest and plain; not constrained by small points; also lacked village-corner praise.
translations.idiomatic: He was plain and blunt, careless of niceties, and won no local acclaim.

sentences.zh: 王莽末，天下大亂，延常嬰甲胄，擁衛親族，扞禦抄盜，賴其全者甚眾。

translations.literal: At the end of Wang Mang, the realm was greatly chaotic; Yan constantly wore armor and helmet, embraced and guarded kinsmen, defended against raiding bandits; those who relied on him to be preserved were very many.
translations.idiomatic: When Wang Mang fell and chaos spread, Yu Yan kept his armor on, shielded his kin from pillagers, and saved many lives.

sentences.zh: 延從女弟年在孩乳，其母不能活之，棄於溝中，延聞其號聲，哀而收之，養至成人。

translations.literal: Yan's cousin younger sister was in age infant suckling; her mother could not keep her alive, discarded her in a ditch; Yan hearing her cry voice, pitied and gathered her, raised her to adulthood.
translations.idiomatic: His infant cousin was abandoned in a ditch because her mother could not feed her; Yu Yan heard her cries, took her in, and raised her to adulthood.

sentences.zh: 建武初，仕執金吾府，除細陽令。

translations.literal: At the beginning of Jianwu, served in Bearer of the Mace office; appointed magistrate of Xiyang.
translations.idiomatic: Early in Jianwu he joined the Bearer of the Mace's staff and was named magistrate of Xiyang.

sentences.zh: 每至歲時伏臘，輒休遣徒系，各使歸家，並感其恩德，應期而還。

translations.literal: Whenever reaching yearly seasons Fu and La festivals, always rested and sent off convicts bound, each caused to return home; together they felt his grace and virtue; answered the period and returned.
translations.idiomatic: At each summer and winter sacrifice he sent prisoners home on leave; grateful for his kindness, they always returned on time.

sentences.zh: 有囚於家被病，自載詣獄，既至而死，延率掾史，殯於門外，百姓感悅之。

translations.literal: There was a prisoner at home who fell ill; self-loaded went to prison; when arrived then died; Yan led clerks and secretaries, buried outside the gate; the hundred surnames felt and rejoiced in him.
translations.idiomatic: One prisoner fell ill at home yet had himself carried back to jail and died on arrival; Yu Yan led his staff in mourning him outside the gates—the people loved him for it.

sentences.zh: 後去官還鄉裏，太守富宗聞延名，召署功曹。

translations.literal: Later he left office and returned to village; Governor Fu Zong heard Yan's name, summoned and appointed him merit assessor.
translations.idiomatic: After he resigned and went home, Governor Fu Zong, having heard his reputation, named him merit assessor.

sentences.zh: 宗性奢靡，車服器物，多不中節。

translations.literal: Zong's nature was extravagant; carriages, dress, tools, and objects mostly did not hit the measure.
translations.idiomatic: Fu Zong lived lavishly: his carriages, dress, and furnishings were far beyond what was proper.

sentences.zh: 延諫曰：『昔晏嬰輔齊，鹿裘不完，季文子相魯，妾不衣帛，以約失之者鮮矣。

translations.literal: Yan remonstrated saying: 'Formerly Yan Ying aided Qi—his deer-fur cloak was never whole; Ji Wenzi ministered Lu while his concubines wore no silk. Few who err through restraint come to grief.'
translations.idiomatic: Yu Yan urged him: 'Yan Ying served Qi in a patched deer cloak; Ji Wenzi ruled Lu while his concubines wore no silk—few who live plainly ever lose their footing.'

sentences.zh: 』宗不悅，延即辭退。

translations.literal: ' Zong was not pleased; Yan immediately resigned and withdrew.
translations.idiomatic: ' Fu Zong took offense and Yu Yan resigned at once.

sentences.zh: 居有頃，宗果以侈從被誅，臨當伏刑，攬涕而嘆曰：『恨不用功曹虞延之諫！

translations.literal: After dwelling a short while, Zong indeed because of extravagant following was executed; when about to suffer punishment, grasping tears sighed saying: 'I regret not using merit assessor Yu Yan's remonstrance!
translations.idiomatic: Soon afterward Fu Zong was executed for extravagance. On the block he wept and cried, 'If only I had listened to Yu Yan!'

sentences.zh: 』光武聞而奇之。

translations.literal: ' Guangwu heard and deemed him extraordinary.
translations.idiomatic: ' Guangwu heard the story and marked Yu Yan as remarkable.

sentences.zh: 二十年東巡，路過小黃，高帝母昭靈後園陵在焉，時延為部督郵，詔呼引見，問園陵之事。

translations.literal: In the twentieth year eastern tour, passing Xiaohuang—Gaodi's mother Empress Zhaoling's garden tomb was there; at the time Yan was departmental postal inspector; edict called him led to audience, asked garden tomb matters.
translations.idiomatic: On his eastern tour in year 20 the emperor passed Xiaohuang, where Gaozu's mother the Empress Dowager Zhaoling lay buried. Yu Yan was serving as inspector; the emperor summoned him to ask about the tomb precinct.

sentences.zh: 延進止從容，占拜可觀，其陵樹株蘗，皆諳其數，俎豆犧牲，頗曉其禮。

translations.literal: Yan's advancing and stopping was leisurely; divining and bowing were observable; its mound trees stumps and sprouts, all knew their numbers; stands and vessels sacrificial victims, rather understood their ritual.
translations.idiomatic: Yu Yan moved and answered with poise; he knew every tree and shoot on the burial mound and the ritual of vessels and offerings.

sentences.zh: 帝善之，敕延從駕到魯。

translations.literal: The emperor praised him; ordered Yan to follow carriage to Lu.
translations.idiomatic: Pleased, the emperor had Yu Yan ride with him to Lu.

sentences.zh: 還經封丘城門，門下小，不容羽蓋，帝怒，使撻侍禦史，延因下見引咎，以為罪在督郵。

translations.literal: Returning passed Fengqiu city gate; the gate below was small, did not accommodate feather canopy; emperor angry, caused flogging attendant imperial clerk; Yan thereupon descended audience and drew blame on himself, held that guilt lay with the postal inspector.
translations.idiomatic: On the return through Fengqiu the gate was too low for the imperial umbrella-carriage. The emperor flogged his carriage attendant; Yu Yan stepped forward and took the blame on himself as local postal inspector.

sentences.zh: 言辭激揚，有感帝意，乃制誥曰：『以陳留督郵虞延故，貰禦史罪。

translations.literal: Words and phrases were stirring; had moving emperor's intent; then issued edict saying: 'Because of Chenliu postal inspector Yu Yan, pardon imperial clerk's crime.
translations.idiomatic: His honest plea moved the throne, which proclaimed: 'For Chenliu postal inspector Yu Yan's sake, spare the attendant.'

sentences.zh: 』延從送車駕西盡郡界，賜錢及劍帶佩刀還郡，於是聲名遂振。

translations.literal: ' Yan followed sending carriage west to the end of commandery border; granted cash and sword belt waist knife returning to commandery; from this reputation then shook.
translations.idiomatic: ' Yu Yan escorted the train to the commandery border, received cash, sword belt, and dagger, and returned—his name rang out thereafter.

sentences.zh: 二十三年，司徒玉況辟焉。

translations.literal: In the twenty-third year Minister of Education Yu Kuang summoned him.
translations.idiomatic: In year 23 Minister of Education Yu Kuang recruited him.

sentences.zh: 時元正朝賀，帝望而識延，遣小黃門馳問之，即日召拜公車令。

translations.literal: At that time New Year's first-month court congratulation, emperor looking recognized Yan, sent junior Yellow Gate galloping to ask him; same day summoned appointed carriage gate superintendent.
translations.idiomatic: At New Year's audience the emperor spotted Yu Yan among the crowd, sent a eunuch to fetch him, and the same day named him superintendent of the Carriage Gate.

sentences.zh: 明年，遷洛陽令。

translations.literal: The next year transferred to Luoyang magistrate.
translations.idiomatic: The following year he became magistrate of Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 是時，陰氏有客馬成者，常為奸盜，延收考之。

translations.literal: At this time the Yin clan had a retainer Ma Cheng who constantly acted villainous theft; Yan seized and examined him.
translations.idiomatic: A hanger-on of the Yin clan named Ma Cheng was a habitual thief; Yu Yan arrested and interrogated him.

sentences.zh: 陰氏屢請，獲一書輒加篣二百。

translations.literal: The Yin clan repeatedly pleaded; each time obtaining one letter then added two hundred strokes of the bamboo.
translations.idiomatic: Each time the Yins interceded with a letter, Yu Yan added two hundred strokes of the paddle.

sentences.zh: 信陽侯陰就乃訴帝，譖延多所冤枉。

translations.literal: Marquis of Xinyang Yin Jiu then complained to the emperor, slandered Yan had many wrongful injustice.
translations.idiomatic: Marquis of Xinyang Yin Jiu appealed to the emperor that Yu Yan was framing the innocent.

sentences.zh: 帝乃臨禦道之館，親錄囚徒。

translations.literal: The emperor then attended the lodge by the imperial way, personally recorded prisoners.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor moved to a lodge on the imperial avenue and reviewed the prisoners himself.

sentences.zh: 延陳其獄狀可論者在東，無理者居西。

translations.literal: Yan arrayed them: those whose prison cases could be debated were east; those without pattern dwelt west.
translations.idiomatic: Yu Yan lined them up with plausible defenses on the east and doubtful cases on the west.

sentences.zh: 成乃回欲趨東，延前執之，謂曰：『爾人之巨蠹，久依城社，不畏熏燒。

translations.literal: Cheng then turned wishing to hurry east; Yan stepped forward seized him, said to him: 'You are a great worm among men; long relying on wall and earth god; not fearing smoke and burning.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Cheng tried to slip east; Yu Yan seized him and said, 'You are a public pest hiding behind patronage like a rat in a wall—wait until we smoke you out.'

sentences.zh: 今考實未竟，宜當盡法！

translations.literal: Now examination of substance not yet finished; ought to apply full law!
translations.idiomatic: The inquiry is not finished—you deserve the full penalty!

sentences.zh: 』成大呼稱枉，陛戟郎以戟刺延，叱使置之。

translations.literal: Cheng greatly shouted calling wrong; halberd attendant at the palace steps thrust at Yan with the halberd; the emperor shouted to release him.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Cheng howled injustice until a halberd guard struck at Yu Yan—the emperor ordered him to stand down.

sentences.zh: 帝知延不私，謂成曰：『汝犯王法，身自取之！

translations.literal: The emperor knew Yan was not partial, said to Cheng: 'You violated royal law; body self fetched it!
translations.idiomatic: Seeing Yu Yan's impartiality, the emperor told Ma Cheng, 'You broke the law—your fate is your own doing!'

sentences.zh: 』呵使速去。

translations.literal: He shouted, ordering him to leave at once.
translations.idiomatic: He shouted at Ma Cheng to withdraw.

sentences.zh: 後數日伏誅，於是外戚斂手，莫敢幹法。

translations.literal: After several days suffered execution; from this outer kin gathered hands; none dared offend law.
translations.idiomatic: Days later Ma Cheng was executed; the imperial in-laws pulled back their hands and no one dared flout the law.

sentences.zh: 在縣三年，遷南陽太守。

translations.literal: In the county three years, transferred to Nanyang governor.
translations.idiomatic: After three years as magistrate he became governor of Nanyang.

sentences.zh: 永平初，有新野功曹鄧衍，以外戚小侯每豫朝會，而容姿趨步，有出於眾，顯宗目之，顧左右曰：『朕之儀貌，豈若此人！

translations.literal: At the beginning of Yongping there was Xinye merit assessor Deng Yan; as a minor marquis by imperial marriage he often attended court assembly; his bearing and gait stood out from the crowd; Emperor Xiaozong eyed him and said to those beside him: 'Does my deportment match such a man!
translations.idiomatic: Early in Yongping, Deng Yan of Xinye—married into the imperial clan as a minor marquis—attended court often enough that Emperor Ming noticed his striking looks and carriage and asked his attendants, 'Do I present myself half so well as this man?'

sentences.zh: 』特賜輿馬衣服。

translations.literal: ' Specially granted carriage, horses, and clothes.
translations.idiomatic: ' He showered Deng Yan with carriages, horses, and robes.

sentences.zh: 延以衍雖有容儀而無實行，未嘗加禮。

translations.literal: Yan held that though Yan had bearing and deportment he lacked solid conduct; never applied ritual courtesy.
translations.idiomatic: Yu Yan thought him handsome but hollow and never showed him special courtesy.

sentences.zh: 帝既異之，乃詔衍令自稱南陽功曹詣闕。

translations.literal: The emperor since deemed him unusual, then edicted Yan ordering him to style himself Nanyang merit assessor and go to palace gate.
translations.idiomatic: Puzzled by Yu Yan's coldness, the emperor ordered Deng Yan to present himself at court as if still Nanyang's merit assessor.

sentences.zh: 既到，拜郎中，遷玄武司馬。

translations.literal: When arrived, appointed gentleman consultant; transferred to Xuanwu marshal.
translations.idiomatic: On arrival Deng Yan was named gentleman consultant and then marshal of the Xuanwu quarter.

sentences.zh: 衍在職不服父喪，帝聞之，乃嘆曰：『「知人則哲，惟帝難之。

translations.literal: Yan in office did not observe mourning for his father; when the emperor heard, he sighed and said: '"To know men is wise—only the emperor finds it hard."'
translations.idiomatic: When Deng Yan ignored mourning for his father, the emperor sighed, "To know men is wisdom—and it is hardest for the one who sits on the throne."

sentences.zh: 」信哉斯言！

translations.literal: " Truly so this saying!
translations.idiomatic: True words, those!

sentences.zh: 』衍慚而退，由是以延為明。

translations.literal: Yan ashamed and withdrew; from this Yan was held enlightened.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Yan withdrew in shame, and men judged Yu Yan the clear-sighted one.

sentences.zh: 三年，征代趙憙為太尉；

translations.literal: In the third year, summoned to replace Zhao Xi as grand commandant;
translations.idiomatic: In year 3 he was summoned to succeed Zhao Xi as grand commandant;

sentences.zh: 八年，代範遷為司徒，歷位二府，十餘年無異政績。

translations.literal: In the eighth year, replaced Fan Qian as minister of education; passing through two offices, more than ten years without exceptional administrative traces.
translations.idiomatic: In year 8 he succeeded Fan Qian as minister of education; for a decade at the summit of government he left no striking mark.

sentences.zh: 會楚王英謀反，陰氏欲中傷之，使人私之楚謀告延，延以英藩戚至親，不然其言，又欲辟幽州從事公孫弘，以弘交通楚王而止，並不奏聞。

translations.literal: It happened the king of Chu, Ying, plotted rebellion; the Yin clan wished to injure him, sent someone secretly to use Chu's plot to inform Yan; Yan because Ying was close kin of the imperial house, did not credit the report; also wished to summon Youzhou staffer Gongsun Hong, but because Hong was in contact with the king of Chu desisted; together he did not memorialize.
translations.idiomatic: When Liu Ying, king of Chu, plotted revolt, the Yins tried to entrap Yu Yan by secretly passing him word of the plot. Yan would not believe it—Ying was too close in blood to the throne—and he dropped a summons for Youzhou staffer Gongsun Hong when he learned Hong was in contact with the prince. He reported nothing to the court.

sentences.zh: 及英事發覺，詔書切讓，延遂自殺。

translations.literal: When Ying's affair was discovered and revealed, edict script sternly blamed; Yan thereupon killed himself.
translations.idiomatic: When Ying's plot surfaced, a blistering edict blamed Yu Yan, who took his own life.

sentences.zh: 家至清貧，子孫不免寒餧。

translations.literal: House extremely clear and poor; children and grandchildren did not avoid cold and hunger.
translations.idiomatic: His household was bitterly poor; even his descendants went cold and hungry.

sentences.zh: 延從曾孫放，字子仲。

translations.literal: Yan's collateral great-grandson Fang, courtesy name Zizhong.
translations.idiomatic: Yu Fang, a collateral descendant of Yu Yan, styled Zizhong.

sentences.zh: 少為太尉楊震門徒，及震被讒自殺，順帝初，放詣闕追訟震罪，由是知名。

translations.literal: In youth he was a disciple of Grand Commandant Yang Zhen; when Zhen was slandered and killed himself, at the beginning of Emperor Shun, Fang went to the palace gate to pursue suit of Zhen's crime; from this became known.
translations.idiomatic: He had studied under Grand Commandant Yang Zhen; after Yang died under slander, Fang petitioned at the capital to clear his teacher's name at the start of Emperor Shun's reign and won fame.

sentences.zh: 桓帝時為尚書，以議誅大將軍梁冀功封都亭侯，後為司空，坐水災免。

translations.literal: In Emperor Huan's time he was Master of Writing; by merit of deliberating execution of Grand General Liang Ji he was enfeoffed marquis of Duting; later was Minister of Works; sat for flood disaster dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Huan he rose to Master of Writing and was made Marquis of Duting for his part in bringing down Liang Ji; he later served as Minister of Works until floods cost him his post.

sentences.zh: 性疾惡宦官，遂為所陷，靈帝初，與長樂少府李膺等俱以黨事誅。

translations.literal: By nature he hated evil eunuchs; therefore was framed by them; at the beginning of Emperor Ling, together with Prefect of Changle Li Ying and others alike by faction affair executed.
translations.idiomatic: He despised the eunuchs and they ruined him; early in Emperor Ling's reign he was executed with Li Ying and others in the faction cases.

sentences.zh: 鄭弘字巨君，會稽山陰人也。

translations.literal: Zheng Hong, courtesy name Junju, was a man of Shanyin in Kuaiji.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Hong, styled Junju, came from Shanyin in Kuaiji commandery.

sentences.zh: 從祖吉，宣帝時為西域都護。

translations.literal: His senior cousin Ji in Emperor Xuan's time was protector of the Western Regions.
translations.idiomatic: His grandfather's cousin Zheng Ji had been protector of the Western Regions under Emperor Xuan.

sentences.zh: 弘少為鄉嗇夫，太守第五倫行春，見而深奇之，召署督郵，舉孝廉。

translations.literal: Hong in youth was village bailiff; Governor Diwu Lun on spring circuit saw him and deeply deemed him extraordinary, summoned and appointed postal inspector, raised filial and incorrupt.
translations.idiomatic: As a young village bailiff he impressed Governor Diwu Lun on a spring inspection tour; Lun named him postal inspector and nominated him filial and incorrupt.

sentences.zh: 弘師同郡河東太守焦貺。

translations.literal: Hong took as teacher Jiao Kuang, governor of Hedong of the same commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Hong studied under his fellow townsman Jiao Kuang, governor of Hedong.

sentences.zh: 楚王英謀反發覺，以疏引貺，貺被收捕，疾病於道亡沒，妻子閉系詔獄，掠考連年。

translations.literal: King of Chu Ying's rebellion plot was discovered; by memorial Kuang was implicated; Kuang was seized and captured; ill on the road he died while fleeing; wife and children shut bound in edict prison; flog-examined successive years.
translations.idiomatic: When Liu Ying of Chu's plot surfaced, a memorial named Jiao Kuang; Kuang was arrested but died on the road while fleeing illness, leaving wife and children shackled in the imperial jail for years of torture.

sentences.zh: 諸生故人懼相連及，皆改變名姓，以逃其禍，弘獨髡頭負鈇锧，詣闕上章，為貺訟罪。

translations.literal: Students and old friends feared mutual linkage and involvement; all changed names and surnames to escape its calamity; Hong alone shaved head, bore axe and chopping-block, went to palace gate and submitted a memorial, for Kuang pleaded his innocence.
translations.idiomatic: Students and friends hid under aliases to escape guilt; Zheng Hong alone shaved his head, carried an executioner's block to the palace, and filed a memorial defending Jiao Kuang.

sentences.zh: 顯宗覺悟，即赦其家屬，弘躬送貺喪及妻子還鄉裏，由是顯名。

translations.literal: Emperor Xiaozong awakened and understood; immediately pardoned his family members; Hong personally escorted Kuang's corpse and wife and children returning to village; from this name became manifest.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Ming took his meaning, freed the family, and Hong personally escorted Kuang's body and kin home—a deed that made his name known.

sentences.zh: 拜為騶令，政有仁惠，民稱蘇息。

translations.literal: Appointed magistrate of Zou; governance had kindness and grace; the people called recovery and rest.
translations.idiomatic: As magistrate of Zou he ruled with mercy; the people said they could breathe again.

sentences.zh: 遷淮陰太守。

translations.literal: Transferred to governor of Huaiyin.
translations.idiomatic: He was promoted to governor of Huaiyin.

sentences.zh: 四遷，建初初，為尚書令。

translations.literal: Four transfers; at the beginning of Jianchu, became Master of Writing.
translations.idiomatic: After four promotions he became chief Master of Writing early in the Jianchu era.

sentences.zh: 舊制，尚書郎限滿補縣長令史丞尉。

translations.literal: Old system: when Masters of Writing gentlemen completed their term limit, they were assigned as county magistrates, chief clerks, assistants, or junior magistrates.
translations.idiomatic: Formerly, gentlemen of the Masters of Writing who finished their tour were posted as county magistrates, chief clerks, or assistants.

sentences.zh: 弘奏以為臺職雖尊，而酬賞甚薄，至於開選，多無樂者，請使郎補千石令，令史為長。

translations.literal: Hong memorialized holding that though terrace duties were honored, reward and emoluments were very thin; when it came to opening selection, many had no desire for it; he requested that gentlemen be assigned as magistrates of two thousand piculs and chief clerks as county elders.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Hong argued that palace posts carried thin rewards, so few welcomed promotion under the old rule; he asked that gentlemen be posted as commandery-level magistrates and chief clerks as county elders.

sentences.zh: 帝從其議。

translations.literal: The emperor followed his deliberation.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor accepted his proposal.

sentences.zh: 弘前後所陳有補益王政者，皆著之南宮，以為故事。

translations.literal: Hong before and after what he stated had benefit to royal governance—all were recorded at Southern Palace, taken as precedent.
translations.idiomatic: His memorials that aided governance were filed in the Southern Palace as precedent.

sentences.zh: 出為平原相，征拜侍中。

translations.literal: Went out as chancellor of Pingyuan; summoned appointed attendant at the palace gates.
translations.idiomatic: He served as chancellor of Pingyuan and was recalled as palace attendant.

sentences.zh: 建初八年，代鄭眾為大司農。

translations.literal: In the eighth year of Jianchu, replaced Zheng Zhong as Minister of Agriculture.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianchu 8 he succeeded Zheng Zhong as Minister of Agriculture.

sentences.zh: 舊交阯七郡貢獻轉運，皆從東冶泛海而至，風波艱阻，沈溺相系。

translations.literal: Formerly Jiaozhi seven commanderies tribute transport, all from Dongye floated sea and arrived; wind waves hardship obstruction; drowning sinking linked one after another.
translations.idiomatic: Tribute from the seven Jiaozhi commanderies once sailed from Dongye across open sea—storms sank ship after ship.

sentences.zh: 弘奏開零陵、桂陽嶠道，於是夷通，至今遂為常路。

translations.literal: Hong memorialized opening Lingling, Guiyang ridge roads; from this barbarians communicated; down to today then became regular route.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Hong opened the ridge roads through Lingling and Guiyang; the interior could trade with the south by land—still the usual route today.

sentences.zh: 在職二年，所息省三億萬計。

translations.literal: In office two years, what was rested and saved three hundred million calculated.
translations.idiomatic: In two years he saved the treasury on the order of three hundred million cash.

sentences.zh: 時歲天下遭旱，邊方有警，人食不足，而帑藏殷積。

translations.literal: At that time year the realm suffered drought; border regions had alarms; people's food not sufficient; yet treasury stores abundant piled.
translations.idiomatic: Drought gripped the empire and the borders stirred while people went hungry—yet the imperial vaults overflowed.

sentences.zh: 弘又奏宜省貢獻，減徭費，以利饑人。

translations.literal: Hong again memorialized it was fitting to reduce tribute, decrease corvée expense, to benefit hungry people.
translations.idiomatic: He urged cuts to tribute and labor levies to aid the starving.

sentences.zh: 帝順其議。

translations.literal: The emperor complied with his deliberation.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor agreed.

sentences.zh: 論曰：孔子稱『可與立，未可與權』。

translations.literal: The appraisal says: Confucius called 'May stand with him, not yet may weigh circumstances with him.'
translations.idiomatic: The historian remarks: Confucius said, 'You can stand firm with some men—not weigh shifting circumstances with every one.'

sentences.zh: 權也者，反常者也。

translations.literal: Weighing circumstances is departing from the norm.
translations.idiomatic: Expediency means departing from the usual rule.

sentences.zh: 將從反常之事，必資非常之會，使夫舉無違妄，誌行名全。

translations.literal: About to follow abnormal affairs, must rely on extraordinary conjunctions, causing those undertakings without violation or recklessness, aspiration and conduct name preserved whole.
translations.idiomatic: Acting outside the norm calls for extraordinary timing—only then can the deed avoid folly and leave ambition, deed, and reputation intact.

sentences.zh: 周章身非負圖之托，德乏萬夫之望，主無絕天之釁，

translations.literal: Zhou Zhang bore no regent's charge like the Duke of Zhou; virtue fell short of the multitude's hopes; the ruler showed no crime against Heaven's mandate,
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Zhang bore no Duke-of-Zhou mandate; he lacked popular esteem; the emperor gave Heaven no cause for revolt,

sentences.zh: 地有既安之埶，而創慮於難圖，希功於理絕，不已悖乎！

translations.literal: earth had already settled posture, yet he devised schemes on hard-to-plan matters, hoped achievement where principle was cut off—is it not already perverse!
translations.idiomatic: the realm already rested secure—yet he plotted the impossible and hoped for success where cause was dead—was that not folly?

sentences.zh: 如令君器易以下議，即鬥筲必能叨天業，狂夫豎臣亦自奮矣。

translations.literal: If high office yields easily to counsel from below, then petty measure could seize heaven's enterprise; madmen and petty ministers too would exert themselves.
translations.idiomatic: If high office bends to petty counsel, every small talent will grasp at the Mandate and cranks will rush forward.

sentences.zh: 孟軻有言曰：『有伊尹之心則可，無伊尹之心則篡矣。

translations.literal: Meng Ke had words saying: 'Having Yi Yin's heart then acceptable; lacking Yi Yin's heart then usurpation.
translations.idiomatic: Mencius said, 'With Yi Yin's intent it is permissible; without it, it is usurpation.'

sentences.zh: 』於戲，方來之人戒之哉！

translations.literal: ' Alas, those who come after—guard against it!
translations.idiomatic: ' Alas—let those who come after take warning!'

sentences.zh: 贊曰：朱定北州，激成寵尤。

translations.literal: The encomium says: Zhu settled northern province; incited completion Chong's fault.
translations.idiomatic: Verse says: Zhu Fu steadied Youzhou but baited Peng Chong into rebellion.

sentences.zh: 魴用降帑，延感歸囚。

translations.literal: Fang used surrendering treasury; Yan moved returning prisoners.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Fang drew grain from the treasury to win surrender; Yu Yan's grace sent prisoners home on time.

sentences.zh: 鄭、竇怨偶，代相為仇，周章反道，小智大謀。

translations.literal: Zheng, Dou paired enmity; generation succeeding became foes; Zhou Zhang opposed the Way; small wisdom great plotting.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Hong and the Dou clan were paired in strife; generation after generation turned hostile; Zhou Zhang defied the Way—petty wit, vaulting plot.
